what_it_does: An HTML living-specification page for the Glyphic System: it documents a compositional icon/glyph model, embeds verbatim source briefs, decomposes the model into orthogonal facets, surveys related codebase systems, proposes a build plan, and renders live facet specimens and a cumulative walk matrix using the design-system registries.
observations: Self-contained HTML page with embedded CSS and JavaScript, linking to ../colors_and_type.css, ../tokens/*.css, and many ../axes/*.js / ../assets/icons/*.js modules.
Reproduces two long verbatim messages from Vi/Tim as the source briefs, then decomposes them into precise design-system vocabulary.
Defines a Glyphic as a stack of independent facets: Form, Symbol, Fill, Colour, Texture, Motion, Size, Depth.
Includes live-rendered facet table and cumulative walk matrix generated by window.CV_SHAPES and window.CV_GLYPHIC.
Frames the Glyphic as the first worked example of a universal component grammar with slots, sockets, declarations, and registry integration.
Marks several implementation steps as BUILT: registry home, taxonomy, schemas, meaning profiles, React component, Type registration, modifier facets, AI capabilities.
Ends with open questions for markup/feedback and a section on universal axis systems (CV_AXES).
Uses data-screen-label attributes on sections to support contextual commenting.
Contains local CSS keyframe animations for motion-facet demonstrations.
Does not implement the registries itself; it references and depends on external globals.
standouts: The page doubles as a design brief, build-status dashboard, and interactive proof of the facet-composition model.
It explicitly maps informal user language (slots, sockets, attachments) onto the existing registry API and type-declaration shape.
Live glyphic specimens in the browser are used as evidence that the compositional model is already real.
conventions: Follows design-system page conventions: masthead, section numbering, lede paragraphs, callouts, code blocks, research cards, steps, open questions.
Uses CSS custom properties and a warm, low-contrast colour palette consistent with the surrounding system.
Uses semantic data-screen-label attributes for section-level markup/commenting.
Breaks the convention of concise docs by embedding very long verbatim user messages and extensive proposal text.
Mixes documentation, live demos, and implementation-status claims in a single file.
concerns: Very long inline verbatim text makes the file large and harder to maintain.
Live rendering depends on many external scripts whose contents are not shown; failures would break specimens silently.
Local CSS keyframes for motion facets may duplicate definitions in tokens/motion.css or assets/icons/cv-glyphic.css.
BUILT status claims cannot be verified from this file alone.
Some demo logic uses hardcoded colour/depth recipes rather than reading purely from token sources.
notes: A dense, mark-up-able living spec that treats an icon as a multi-facet compositional glyph and uses live browser rendering to prove the model.
questions: Which of the linked external modules exist and export the expected globals (CV_SHAPES, CV_GLYPHIC, CV_ICONS, etc.)?
How is the BUILT status kept in sync with the actual implementation state?
What happens to the live specimens if the external scripts fail to load?
Is this page intended only for design review, or also as runtime documentation for consumers?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a living spec for markup and direction; actual content is a hybrid of spec, verbatim source archive, build-status dashboard, and interactive proof — mostly aligned, but broader than a pure spec.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth via registries.
Generative composition instead of hand-drawn assets.
Universal component grammar: slots, sockets, declarations.
Self-similar system architecture from atom to deck.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish the Glyphic as the atomic unit of a generative design system, show that the existing codebase already supports the pattern, and provide a steerable plan for completing and extending the build.
novelty: Distinct in formalising an icon as a multi-facet compositional glyph with slots/sockets/declarations, and in using a design-system HTML page as both a readable spec and a live build-status dashboard.
contribution: This file contributes a readable, mark-up-able living specification that articulates the Glyphic model, maps it to existing registry and axis infrastructure, and provides live visual proof of compositional facets — useful for aligning design intent with implementation status.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML living specification that documents the Glyphic System as a multi-facet compositional glyph model, embeds source briefs, surveys related codebase systems, proposes a build plan, and renders live facet specimens via the design-system registries.
intention_for_embedding: To give Vi a mark-up-able design-system document that captures the Glyphic concept, decomposes the brief, reports build progress, and solicits directional feedback.
